title: Accro Bioscience: $50 Million Series C Raised To Advance Immune Mediated Disease Pipeline
summary:
Accro Bioscience announced the closing of a $50 million Series C financing round led by OrbiMed, with participation from TCG Crossover (TCGX), LAV, Cenova Capital, and existing investors Shenzhen Capital Group (SCGC) and Oriza Holdings.
The post Accro Bioscience: $50 Million Series C Raised To Advance Immune Mediated Disease Pipeline appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Pennant Group: Arizona Senior Living Expansion With Tucson Memory Care Acquisition
summary:
The Pennant Group announced the acquisition of the real estate and operations of Copper Canyon Memory Care, an established senior living community in Tucson, Arizona. The acquisition expands Pennant’s Arizona senior living operations by 40 units and strengthens the company’s presence in the state’s memory care market.
The post Pennant Group: Arizona Senior Living Expansion With Tucson Memory Care Acquisition appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Walker & Dunlop: $128.5 Million Financing Arranged For The Arno Luxury Community In Houston’s River Oaks
summary:
Walker & Dunlop announced it arranged $128.5 million in acquisition financing for The Arno, a 168-unit luxury residential community located in Houston’s River Oaks neighborhood.
The post Walker & Dunlop: $128.5 Million Financing Arranged For The Arno Luxury Community In Houston’s River Oaks appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Kin Health: $9 Million Raised To Build Patient-Owned Health Records From Doctor Conversations
summary:
Kin Health, a consumer health platform founded by practicing physicians, announced a $9 million seed round to help patients better understand and retain information from their medical appointments. The funding round was led by Maveron, with participation from Town Hall Ventures, Flex Capital, Eniac Ventures, Pear VC, and several healthcare and technology investors.
The post Kin Health: $9 Million Raised To Build Patient-Owned Health Records From Doctor Conversations appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Watson’s: Carolinas Expansion Accelerates With Viridien Patio + Fireplace Acquisition
summary:
Watson’s, one of the nation’s largest retailers of home recreational products and furnishings, announced the acquisition of Charlotte-based Viridien Patio + Fireplace, a leading destination in the Carolinas for high-end outdoor furniture, outdoor kitchens, fireplace, and hearth products.
The post Watson’s: Carolinas Expansion Accelerates With Viridien Patio + Fireplace Acquisition appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Lauxera Capital Partners: €520 Million Fund Raised For European Healthtech Expansion
summary:
Lauxera Capital Partners announced the successful close of its Lauxera Growth II fund at €520 million, surpassing its €500 million hard cap and nearly doubling the size of the firm’s debut fund. The oversubscribed fund was finalized less than 18 months after its first closing and reflects strong support from both returning and new limited partners, including Morgan Stanley Investment Management, Bpifrance, EIF (European Investment Fund), Sagard, Flextone, Swen Capital Partners, and Malakoff Humanis.

title: Nourish: $100 Million Series C Raised For AI-Native Metabolic Health Clinic
summary:
Nourish announced a $100 million Series C round, bringing the company’s total funding to $215 million as it expands its AI-powered metabolic healthcare platform focused on reversing chronic disease. The funding round was led by Menlo Ventures, with participation from Thrive Capital, Index Ventures, J.P. Morgan Growth Equity Partners, Maverick Ventures, Y Combinator, BoxGroup, Atomico, Daybreak, and Operator Partners. As part of the investment, Menlo Ventures partner J.P. Sanday will join Nourish’s board.

title: Lexroom: $50 Million Series B Raised To Expand Legal AI Platform Across Civil Law Europe
summary:
Lexroom announced it raised $50 million in Series B funding led by Left Lane Capital, with participation from Base10 Partners, Eurazeo, Acurio Ventures, Entourage, and View Different. The funding round comes eight months after the company’s $19 million Series A and brings Lexroom’s total funding to more than $73 million.
The post Lexroom: $50 Million Series B Raised To Expand Legal AI Platform Across Civil Law Europe appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Moburst: $11.8 Million Raised From Digital Marketing-Focused Investment Firm Chrysalis Holdings
summary:
Moburst announced it secured an $11.8 million strategic investment from Chrysalis Holdings, a private investment firm focused on financial services and direct marketing industries. The investment includes an equity stake in the agency and expanded services supporting Chrysalis portfolio company NewDay USA.
The post Moburst: $11.8 Million Raised From Digital Marketing-Focused Investment Firm Chrysalis Holdings appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Sony Pictures Television Acquires Majority Stake In 32 Flavors To Expand Premium Unscripted Portfolio
summary:
Sony Pictures Television announced it has acquired a majority interest in Alex Baskin’s production company, 32 Flavors, as the studio continues expanding its premium unscripted programming business.
The post Sony Pictures Television Acquires Majority Stake In 32 Flavors To Expand Premium Unscripted Portfolio appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Scope: $20 Million Raised For AI Inspection Platform Transforming The TIC Industry
summary:
Scope, an AI workflow platform for the testing, inspection and certification (TIC) industry, announced it has raised $20 million in funding led by Index Ventures with participation from Susa Ventures, Entrepreneurs First, and Syndicate 1. The company also attracted notable angel investors including Mehdi Ghissassi, Cedric Pech, Naren Shaam, and Andy Szybalski.
The post Scope: $20 Million Raised For AI Inspection Platform Transforming The TIC Industry appeared first on Pulse 2.0.

title: Decart: $300 Million Raised At Nearly $4 Billion Valuation Raised To Make Switching AI Chips Easier
summary:
Decart announced it has raised $300 million in a Series B funding round led by Radical Ventures and Jordan Jacobs, bringing the company’s total funding to more than $450 million. According to reports, the financing values Decart at approximately $4 billion. The company said that several leading technology companies joined the round as both customers and investors, as Decart continues building infrastructure and real-time world models aimed at accelerating the path toward artificial general intelligence (AGI).
